Empires & Puzzles Costume Flip Review: Donut to the Rescue!
Flip returns with a fresh donut and a massive stat boost. Is the Donut Vendor costume the sweetest treat in your roster? We review his new Cleric form and why he's a top-tier red healer.
Costume Flip Review: Donut Fear, The Healer is Here!
Costume Flip: Serving Hot Donuts and Even Hotter Heals
(affectionately known as the "Donut Vendor") transforms the playful seal into a modern powerhouse. While the base version was charming but frail, this

arrives with updated 2025 stats and an improved skill set that solidifies his role as one of the best utility healers in the game. He trades his lifebuoy for a donut, and honestly, we relate to that upgrade on a spiritual level.

The costume art is pure joy. Flip is now balancing a giant pink frosted donut with sprinkles on his nose and wearing a red-and-white striped vendor vest. It’s ridiculous, it’s campy, and it perfectly fits the lighthearted Beach Party theme. The shift from a life guard to a snack vendor is a hilarious touch. Who needs saving when you have snacks?
Costume Flip brings the heat with

. These are true modern stats. Compared to his base form (which languished with ~830 attack and ~1500 HP), this is a night-and-day difference. He can now comfortably survive hits from top-tier snipers and stay alive to keep your team in the fight.
Special Skill: Donut to the Rescue!
"Donut to the Rescue!" is a strict upgrade over the original skill:
(up from 45%). This is a standard for top-tier healers, restoring a massive chunk of health (often 1200+ HP with his high health pool).
Retains the signature ability for 4 turns. This remains invaluable against defensive setups relying on buffs.
(from 50%). It might seem small, but every percentage point counts when you're relying on tile damage to kill a titan or break a tank.
Average. With his Cleric mana bonuses and a level 23+ mana troop, he becomes functionally faster.
With 2235 base HP and 1241 Defense, his effective health is among the highest for healers, making him a sturdy tank or flank option.
50% of an average 5-star team's HP (approx 2500) is 1250 HP per hero. That is significant sustain.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
Standard Beach Party reduction of DoT. Useful, but not the main selling point.
272 damage over 2 turns. It's slightly more than the base version, but still mostly a nuisance rather than a kill condition. It does help in stripping "Cleanse on damage" effects.
. This is huge. For a healer, taking less damage is always better than having more defense (which can be bypassed). This makes him incredibly tanky and reliable.
Base Flip was niche; Costume Flip is meta. He arrived when Red healers were needing a refresh.

He stands tall against the creep. His stats are competitive with late-2024 and 2025 heroes. The jump from Base to Costume is one of the most significant power-ups we've seen, essentially replacing a 4-star stat line with a top-tier 5-star one.
. He is a top-tier choice for Red stacks in War, a staple for Titans, and excellent in PvP attack.
(Costume) vs Sorcerer (Base). The Manashield talent gives him a chance to resist mana control (like Silence or Mana Cut), which is vastly superior for a healer than the Sorcerer's delay.
The Costume bonus gives hefty stat multipliers to both versions, but the Costume version itself has the far superior base stats.
While both do similar things, the Costume does it with survivability and reliability. There is almost no scenario where you would prefer the Base version over this Costume.
As a Cleric, he has the Manashield talent.
Maximizes his tankiness. He needs to live to heal.
Lower tile damage, but he isn't a primary damage dealer.
path. You want that Manashield to trigger and you want him to absorb as much punishment as possible.

Hard counters defensive buffers (Ludwig, Hathor, etc.).
50% flat heal is reliable and strong.
Resisting mana ailments ensures he casts when needed.
Still susceptible to very fast rush teams if he can't get his tiles.
He heals and buffs, but doesn't remove ailments from your team or buffs from the enemy (though he lets you ignore their buffs, he doesn't remove them).
Essential for Red Titan teams due to Crit + Bypass.
Great on both Attack and Defense (Tank/Flank).
Excellent counter to heavy buff teams.
(Costume) who cleanses and reflects, covering Flip's weakness.
Flip + bypass rangers (like Tetisheri). The entire enemy defense becomes irrelevant.
Flip + Buster + Phenexa. Massive damage output.
Heroes that reduce healing or steal healing (like vampires or sand heroes) hurt his effectiveness.
If he can't cast his buffs, he's just a healer.
If you get this costume, max it immediately. He will be your primary red healer for a very long time.

He is worth the mats. Even if you have Hathor or Garnet, Flip offers a different kind of utility (Offensive Support) that makes him invaluable for Titans and specific attack strategies. He is a prime candidate for Limit Breaking.
“It’s one of those costumes where every aspect is a bit better.”
- The community consensus is that this is a straight, no-downsides upgrade.
“I love his bypass defensive buffs... Great against counter attack heroes.”
- Players consistently praise the bypass mechanic as his standout feature.

Costume Flip takes a fun mechanic and wraps it in a package that can actually survive the modern battlefield. He is no longer just a "Titan hero" or a "niche counter"; he is a legitimate, robust healer who brings offensive firepower to the table. The donut is just the icing on the cake.
A delicious upgrade that makes this seal a must-have.
A detailed review of Costume Flip (Donut Vendor), the 5-star Fire Cleric from the Beach Party family. We analyze his upgraded stats, skills, and meta viability.
